2) Inverse Diffusion Process for Pedestrian T rajectory: Dur-
ing the inverse diffusion modeling process, the act of sampling
from the conditional distribution q(Xt‚ąí1 | Xt ) allows us
to reconstruct the genuine original trajectory by drawing
from a random Gaussian distribution N (0, I). This essentially
involves deriving a real trajectory from a trajectory that
entirely comprises random noise. However, the prediction
of q(Xt‚ąí1 | Xt ) is a nontrivial task due to the neces-
sity of determining the underlying data distribution from
the complete dataset. Therefore, it becomes imperative to
use a learned model pőł to approximate this conditional
probability, facilitating the execution of the inverse diffusion
process.
To formalize this, let us consider the input trajectoryXt. The
conditional probability q(Xt‚ąí1 | Xt ) represents the likelihood
of extrapolating from the current trajectory Xt to the trajectory
at the preceding time step, Xt‚ąí1
